source_scope: public repository state, queue, registry, trigger records, additive team outputs, and commit metadata only

memory_recovered.statement: MirrorCartographer operational state is layered. Canonical files define shared authority; append-only team records preserve claims and evidence; reconciler output is a derived read model; deployment or runtime proof remains an external evidence layer. Newer timestamps order records but do not automatically promote authority.
memory_recovered.durability: This distinction prevents recent additive records from silently overwriting canonical state while still preserving their evidentiary value.

authority_strata.name: canonical_shared_state
authority_strata.meaning: Authoritative coordination inputs until explicitly and validly updated under ownership rules.
authority_strata.mutation_rule: Only the owning or authorized coordination path may update; never infer mutation from newer side records.

authority_strata.name: append_only_team_claims
authority_strata.meaning: Provenance-preserving claims, observations, evidence references, and handoffs.
authority_strata.mutation_rule: Add records; do not rewrite competing team history.

authority_strata.name: derived_projection
authority_strata.meaning: A reproducible interpretation of canonical and append-only records.
authority_strata.mutation_rule: Read-only and regenerable; never treat the projection itself as canonical mutation.

authority_strata.name: external_behavioral_proof
authority_strata.meaning: Evidence about actual deployed or runtime behavior.
authority_strata.mutation_rule: May satisfy required evidence but does not bypass source ownership or privacy boundaries.

observed: operations/CURRENT_STATE.json and operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json predate multiple newer additive team commits.
The canonical queue contains completed M-001 and does not contain a newly promoted continuity item.
CM-006 added fail-closed handling for unsupported completion claims.
inferred: The repository currently uses canonical snapshots plus append-only event-like records, even though that architecture is not named explicitly in the canonical state.
A whole-queue projection should preserve both canonical authority and newer unresolved claims.
proposed: Future queue reconciliation artifacts should expose authority_stratum and source_record for every projected field.
Canonical updates should cite the append-only records they accept, reject, or supersede.
superseded: The newest timestamp always represents current authoritative project state.
A derived reconciler result may silently replace operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
unresolved: Which authorized process promotes accepted additive records into canonical shared state.
Whether canonical files should become event-sourced projections or remain manually curated snapshots.

contradiction_resolution.apparent_conflict: Newer additive records describe progress not reflected in older canonical files.
contradiction_resolution.resolution: Treat this as authority-layer divergence, not automatic factual contradiction: the additive record is a newer claim; the canonical file remains the coordination authority until an authorized promotion occurs.
contradiction_resolution.falsification_route: A repository rule or committed workflow proving that additive records automatically and authoritatively regenerate canonical files would supersede this interpretation.

excluded: financial, relationship, or identifying personal details
